document.title: Positive Experience of a Mobile Radiography Service in Nursing Homes
productModels: Canon DR CXDI-50 G digital flat-panel detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical study reporting that mobile radiography in nursing homes was technically feasible, had good image quality, and reduced unnecessary transport to hospital.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Literature evidence for clinical evaluation of mobile radiography services and portable X-ray use in nursing home settings.
keyPoints: The study included 123 nursing home patients and 241 mobile radiography examinations.
Most examinations were musculoskeletal, with hip/pelvis studies accounting for 57% of all examinations.
All radiographs were obtained using one Canon DR CXDI-50 G digital flat-panel detector attached to a Sedecal Mobile Dragon radiography unit with a Radpro generator.
Only one patient required repeat radiography at the hospital due to underexposed images; in all other cases image quality was adequate for diagnosis.

Twelve of 123 patients had radiographic pathology that required hospital visits for treatment, while 22 patients with musculoskeletal pathology and patients with chest pathology could be treated locally.

entities.name: Canon Europe, Ltd.
entities.evidence: one Canon DR CXDI-50 G digital flat-panel detector (Canon Europe, Ltd., Stockley Park, UK)

entities.name: Sedecal
entities.evidence: Sedecal Mobile Dragon radiography unit with a Radpro generator (Sedecal, Madrid, Spain)

entities.name: Lund municipality
entities.evidence: all 25 nursing homes in the Lund municipality (about 110,000 inhabitants), caring for about 850 patients, participated in the study

concepts.name: Mobile radiography service in nursing homes
concepts.evidence: Mobile radiography in nursing homes is technically feasible, with good image quality.

concepts.name: Digital flat-panel detector
concepts.evidence: All radiographs were obtained with the same mobile unit, consisting of one Canon DR CXDI-50 G digital flat-panel detector

concepts.name: On-site image quality evaluation
concepts.evidence: With the use of the digital detector with instant read-out, it was possible for the radiographer to evaluate image quality on site, both regarding exposure and positioning.

concepts.name: Avoidance of patient transport
concepts.evidence: The most beneficial result was that most patients avoided unnecessary transport back and forth to the hospital

concepts.name: Local treatment after mobile radiography
concepts.pageTarget: Local treatment in nursing homes after radiography
concepts.evidence: the majority of patients could be treated locally

evidence.claim: The article was published in Gerontology 2012, volume 58, pages 107-111.
evidence.pageTarget: Positive Experience of a Mobile Radiography Service in Nursing Homes

evidence.claim: The study aimed to investigate mobile radiography service usefulness from patient and staff perspectives.
evidence.evidenceText: the aim of the current study was to investigate the usefulness of a mobile radiography service for radiological assessment of patients in nursing homes from the patient and staff perspectives.

evidence.claim: The intervention study was performed from September 16, 2008 to September 15, 2009, excluding summer and winter vacation periods.
evidence.evidenceText: This part of the study was performed during the period September 16, 2008 to September 15, 2009 but did not cover the summer and winter vacation periods

evidence.claim: The study included 123 patients and 241 mobile radiography examinations.
evidence.evidenceText: The study included 123 patients (table 1). A mobile radiography service was used to perform 241 different radiography examinations

evidence.claim: The mobile unit used a Canon DR CXDI-50 G digital flat-panel detector attached to a Sedecal Mobile Dragon unit with Radpro generator.
evidence.evidenceText: one Canon DR CXDI-50 G digital flat-panel detector ... attached to a Sedecal Mobile Dragon radiography unit with a Radpro generator

evidence.claim: The equipment weighed 90 kg and was portable enough for mobile use in nursing homes.
evidence.evidenceText: Weighing only 90 kg, it was portable enough for mobile use in nursing homes.

evidence.claim: Most examinations were musculoskeletal, with hip/pelvis at 57%, remaining musculoskeletal at 37%, chest at 4%, and abdomen at 2%.
evidence.evidenceText: Hip or pelvic studies accounted for 57% of all radiography examinations, the remaining musculoskeletal studies for 37%, chest radiographs for 4% and abdominal radiographs for 2%

evidence.claim: Only one patient required repeat radiography at the hospital due to underexposed images.
evidence.evidenceText: One patient only had to have repeat radiography at the hospital due to underexposed images. In all other cases, image quality was adequate for diagnosis.

evidence.claim: Twelve of 123 patients had radiographic pathology that required hospital visits for treatment.
evidence.evidenceText: In this study, only 12 of 123 patients had radiographic pathology that required hospital visits for treatment.

evidence.claim: Nursing home staff responses were positive in all 123 cases.
evidence.evidenceText: The responses from the local staff in the nursing homes were in all 123 cases positive.
evidence.pageTarget: Nursing staff acceptance of mobile radiography

evidence.claim: The article concluded that mobile radiography in nursing homes is technically feasible with good image quality.
evidence.evidenceText: Mobile radiography in nursing homes is technically feasible, with good image quality.
